communities.name: Lethem
communities.infra: Lethem’s water infrastructure comprises six fully operational pumping stations, namely: Old Culver City, New Culver City, Kanaku, Tabatinga, Bon Success, and the Industrial Site. These stations are interconnected through a comprehensive distribution network that provides an impressive 98% coverage of the main township, ensuring that the population has access to treated, chlorinated water around the clock. A seventh pumping station is currently undergoing testing to supply the newly established TrackCHPA housing scheme. This station operates independently from the main network, which continues to efficiently serve the broader population of Lethem.
communities.remarks: Water quality and pump station operations are monitored on a monthly basis.  Several of the existing pump stations in Lethem are producing hard water due to the high mineral content of the aquifers currently in use. As a long-term solution, GWI intends to drill additional wells in targeted areas where geological assessments indicate the potential for softer water sources. This initiative aims to gradually improve overall water quality across the Lethem network and reduce the impacts of hardness on both the distribution system and consumers.
communities.schools: In Lethem, GWI currently provides water to three nursery schools and one primary school, all of which have adequate access to supply. However, the secondary school now under construction does not yet have access to a water system, highlighting a gap that will require attention to ensure reliable service once the facility becomes operational.

Water is stored in elevated metal trestle with storage tanks and distributed through a network that provides coverage to approximately 95% of households. The remaining 5% resides far and scatterd in the outskirts of the village.
communities.remarks: Village coverage is 90%. The other 10% of the residents reside far from the water system. A total of 6 households reside more than 500 meters away and are scattered throughout the village.
communities.schools: Fly Hill have 1 nursery and 1 primary that are receiving adequate water supply from GWI system.

The village has a distribution network that provides household-level service to approximately 80% of the population. A concrete elevated trestle at the school serves as the primary storage facility for the main cluster. GWI is also undertaking a new project to establish a water supply system for an additional cluster of 15 homes, aimed at improving access for residents in the forested areas. GWI is actively monitoring the situation and planning interventions to restore adequate and reliable water service to the affected households.

Following completion of the well, a distribution network will be installed, targeting at least 90% household-level coverage for the village. communities.name: Tiger Pond
communities.infra:
Groundwater from 2 Drilled well, 2 PV systems, Elevated storage, service connection - Tiger Pond Village is served by three drilled wells and one hand-operated well. Prior to 2026, two of the drilled wells were operational and provided the majority of the community's water supply. However, during the recent prolonged dry period, the production yield from both active wells declined significantly, resulting in reduced water availability and limited access for residents.
In response to the situation, Guyana Water Incorporated (GWI) intervened in March 2026 and undertook the drilling of a new well within the community. The project was successfully completed, restoring a reliable water supply to residents and improving the overall performance of the water system. As a result of this intervention, Tigapon Village currently enjoys approximately 98% water coverage, with residents receiving an adequate and consistent supply of potable water.
communities.remarks: The main drilled well system in Tiger Pond has been affected by dry periods, resulting in reduced production. GWI has undertaken rehabilitation works, which have slightly improved output. Two additional systems—a hand-dug well and a second drilled well—complement the main system. Plans are in place to upgrade the second drilled well to enhance water supply for the community
